channel.title: What are your unusual headcanons?
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "What are your unusual headcanons?" - The stranger the better!

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: The numbering of the SCPs is chosen by the O5/Administrator and has a special in-universe meaning. That's why some old SCP have larger numbers and some SCP that can't exist in some canon doesn't have two entries.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: This guy is the Foundation containment specialist, who blow off steam on Youtube.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: My headcanon is that the Foundation is only the most recent version of the Jailors and they go all the way back to the Children of the Night who made Titania's Prison. They contain that which should not be, and the humans first effort was 557, the Ancient Containment Site, which actually contains 2317 Prime under the floor in room 501(the note sadid don't move the stone, and I think we shoudl pay attention to that note, if I'm right, you don't want to know what happens when the stone is removed), with 2317 being merely the back door, and 557-1 was 682. 682 is subordinate to 2317 and they were the orginal SCPs, the things which must be contained and have been contained since the beginning of time. The Foundation has just inherited the role and someday will likely pass on the role to someone else, but the point is, the Jailors are eternal.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
Demotion to D-Class and Keter duty are not real things, they're stories made up to mess with new recruits/scare them into being careful. Likewise, monthly termination is something that more experienced D-class tell the new guys as a form of hazing. These ridiculous things show up in official documentation because sometimes the files are being written by newbies who haven't yet learned that there's no such thing.

Alternatively, the higher-ups want people to believe that demotion to D and Keter duty exist because it keeps them in line. They really do put things like that in the documentation to threaten people, and even though it never actually happens there are still people who believe it's possible. The only way to find out for sure would be to dump some 447 on a dead body and see if you get demoted to D or not (assuming that you survive, of course), and nobody's stupid or crazy enough to try that.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: Honestly, that's kind of cute

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: Members of the Ethics Committee are encouraged to write emails, memos, and personal notes in poetry form to engender empathy and emotion.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: Wow this is literally almost word for word my headcanon for Nobody. Also since Cool War and Snippets from the Serpent both take place in 2013 I have always headcanoned that Nobody intended Claire to be his successor knowing he was going to die but Tangerine also became Nobody because he found the hat.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
Hot take : there is an universe who all the characters of Adult Swim and the Foundation "coexist".

And if you question me about that… Stealing Solidarity, Part 4. That's all I'm gonna say.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: I never did like the idea of Nobody being an organization. I always imagined it more of a batman crossed with doctor who. But your interpretation is actually quite interesting.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
Some thoughts on Nobody:

+ show block
– hide block
  • There's more than one Nobody. It's an organization, and runs more like a spy ring than anything.
  • Nobodies can un-Nobody themselves temporarily— or, if you look at it another way, people can temporarily Nobody themselves. They do this by interacting with a particular talisman; usually it's a hat.
  • When somebody is Nobodied, they become adjacent to reality- still able to interact with it, but slightly out of phase. Any locked door will open at their touch, they can walk through walls, and are essentially unkillable- bullets and swords will pass through them. Pico was able to kill Nobody in The Cool War because they were willing to die.
  • Anyone can become a Nobody if they pick up a Talisman.
  • Current and former Nobodies include:
    • Claire Bright from the Man Who Wasn't There
    • Murphy Law
    • Agent Tangerine from The Cool War
    • The head of the Department of Disinformation within the Foundation
    • An admin of Parawatch
  • Nobodies can access anywhere in the world, as long as it has a staircase. Even one or two steps will suffice. As such, they tend to use the Winchester House as a sort of 'travel hub'.
  • Their headquarters are in Antigonish, Nova Scotia.
  • Their agenda is unclear, but they typically work to preserve the Unknown and keep the secrets of the world; any lives saved or lost in the process are incidental. Their motto is literally "Ignorance is Bliss".
  • They want to keep Humanity in the dark because they're not truly ready for the Big PictureTM and need more time to prepare to face how insignificant they truly are.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
That VPN one is way too believable. It seems like at least half of all anomalous companies and organizations block the Foundation after the first interview where a "customer" asks too many questions.

That anomalous religions one is also interesting. I imagine a large percentage of Foundation personnel have deconverted from their previous religion after only five years of employment due to various experiences with deific and nigh-omnipotent anomalies, and stuff like fifthism with strong physical evidence could have a lot of appeal for those currently undergoing a crisis of belief.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: This is actually really interesting

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
Foundation is Fucked edition:

  • The Foundation doesn't actually recruit the "best in their field". They claim they do, but the most important factor for new hires is whether they're psychologically vulnerable at the time of recruitment.
  • Repeat amnestic usage imparts a unique dissociative disorder that affects approximately 13% of employees and 90% of D-Class, characterized by increased apathy, fatigue, and issues with one's sense of time.
  • The Foundation funds itself primarily through its (largely ex-D-Class staffed) front companies. Its credit score is garbage.
  • It's an open secret that many employees are closet faithfuls to anomalous religions.
  • Divisions centered around internet anomalies are largely forced to VPN hop, as Foundation IP addresses are immediately banned from almost every anomalous website.
  • Foundation administration operates a series of fake trade unions to keep its employees from organizing.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
I find this so cute, and i don't know why.

"Oh yeah, we threw that small ,gibberish speaking, dinosaur into a pocket dimension cuz we were sick of it's shit."

Such a different tone to the always adapting, constantly breaching force of eternity that will live past our lives and legacies to watch all life burn at their own hubris.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
Bunch of stuff about 682:

  • 682 isn't the size of Godzilla, a tiger, or even a dog. It's as big as a housecat, and the only reason its' so lethal is because it's small, fast, and has opposable digits on top of being hard to kill.
  • It looks more like a sauropod than an iguana— long neck, whip-like tail, but has clawed feet.
  • The farmers it killed thought it was a weird baby alligator at first and tried to adopt it.
  • It didn't speak English when it first encountered humans, but instead adapted the ability to speak it, and could do the same for any other language.
  • It has long since been 'terminated', in as much as it can be— a researcher, fed up with the fact that the Foundation was burning resources for termination attempts, literally picked it up, threw it through a portal to a pocket dimension, and scrambled the coordinates so that it could never be found. Since it's technically still in our universe, we don't have a danger of that thing with 507 repeating.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
• Dr. Gears came from SCP-238.
  • Certain American sects of the Church of the Second Hytoth have adopted the Snallygaster into their beliefs, spawning from the use of heptagrams to keep the creature at bay. What the Snallygaster is differs between communities: some believe it steals blood so less can be given to the Koru-teusa; others believe the Snallygaster is an Ortothan who went mad, collecting blood for their gods the wrong way. The belief in the Snallygaster has spread to a few extraterrestrial Ortothan groups who made contact with these Americans.
  • While organizations such as The Black Hunter's Lodge and Змей hold great deals of power within Russian territories, none of them hold a candle to Meat Circus. When para-orgs like the Black Lodge first learned about MC, they tried teaching these "artists" to not act so carelessly and inappropriately around their superiors. But no matter what they did or who they hurt, nothing phased Meat Circus: captured MC members would be butchered and propped up to set an example, when their comrades found the body they would gather around it and start discussing it like it was an art piece; sometimes the dead member joined the conversation too. Those who were tortured for information never cracked, they would just scream in pain while praising/critiquing the method of torture. It was eventually realized that attacking MC cost more money and manpower1 than it was worth.
Footnotes
1. If they were gonna take MC's members then MC might as well return the favor.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: The whole of SCP-1173 is the real containment procedure for SCP-4885.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
That all SCP's are naturally occurring phenomenon that we simply do not understand yet. There is no true original anomaly that created these things, we've always existed alongside the "anomalous".

The Foundation is not nearly as powerful as they are generally depicted, and have to deal with the real life political consequences of their mistakes, as well as having to navigate each foreign state in some way to actually take custody of an anomalous object.

I personally think it's more interesting to think of the Foundation as an NGO that, while probably the most powerful in the context of the paranormal community, still having to abide by the world's rules in some way. It doesn't actually make a lot of sense that the Foundation could just waltz into anywhere without having to make deals/peace with certain actors.

Also, the SCP's are the only actual anomalous objects that exist in the universe. If it doesn't have an article, it's not a thing. It's probably the only way the anomalous could actually be kept a secret for so long, if they're just mega rare like that.

Overall my headcanon is probably a bit entropic and doesn't have a larger meaning behind it, just a world where people exist and do as they do.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: that "when day breaks" could actually happen if the sun goes red gaint and that unlondon was made for when day breaks

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: Black Chamber disinformation because thhe existance of SCP repositories leaked at some point.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: The SCP wiki is a front for the SCP Foundation. Senior staff is in on it.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
I mean, it's pretty simple. As a canon everything person, I don't care about inconsistencies, practicality, or realism.

Basically, the only times I care about any of those things within a single article are when they break verisimilitude (as a weak standard) or when they push suspension of disbelief beyond what the content will bear (as the stronger standard). Or if they hit my pet peeves.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: This is exactly what I think too. Otherwise, it all must just be in Stacie's head. I actually don't get the "canon everything" people, not least because of that article's stellar execution of a remarkably unsatisfying idea.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: I'm going the opposite direction of most headcannons. In mine the Foundation actually has only a few hundred SCPs in containment at any given time, with most anomalies being minor and rare, and the site database is actually a multiversal compilation of SCP Foundations.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
my headcanon is that there is no real SCP-001 and the Foundation exists since the beginning of civilisation.
They just started to actually catalogue there SCP's durring the last centurry and there are propably Milions of uncatalogued SCP's inside some of the older Foundation facilitys.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
ALL ARE TRUE!

+ Buckle Up Kids, It's Gonna Be a Wild Ride
- close

Here's the way I see it, the first SCP-001s are what created the anomalies (except for The Gate Guardian and only because biblical anomalies). The Spiral Path created the first anomaly which was a envelope holding a Sheaf of Papers. This allowed the first of them to discover anomalies. One of them took a group of people to a new place called, The Factory, which then after the discovery creates a few Groups of Interest (M C & D, The FBI's UIU, etc. etc.) which then starts off the Occult Wars.

These occult wars accidentally spawn the Thirty-Six anti-anomalous (individuals that ooze normalcy and neutralizing anomalies) and accidentally attract a individual that knows of the "corruption of reality". He helps recruit a few individuals to help in dealing with the anomalies. He is dubbed, The Administrator, leading them through the war.

During this, they discover an entity that wishes to enter our reality, born from the "corruption" calling himself, The Scarlet King. As things get really heated, it soon becomes all out end of the world. Starting with peace across the world which then degrades into total chaos as they discover that through a side effect, causes the sun to do impossible and weird things.

Almost forgot the Ouroboros events. In searching through the anomalies, they find that even children are affected by this. During the events of these, the Church of the Broken God step forth and create a massive machine that ends up causing massive international destruction. It's only when they notice this that they regret their decision and now plan working with the Foundation on the machine. Meanwhile, an accident causes a researcher to become an anomaly, and knowing how it goes, he helps the Foundation with the war (although he's not as powerful as the ending document claims). Finally, the Chaos Insurgency becomes a sect group established by the Foundation as a rogue's gallery, a suicide squad to handle anomalies. The corruption affected the O5 Council though and that's when they turned on them. This echo follows them into the next event.

A reality reset is set up with the Administrator sacrificing himself, however he did leave behind a few items that would help the Foundation (now if only they remembered where they put the formula for handling anomalies).

The events that took place after the Pandora's Box incident, splitting reality into different "canons" including one that connects to us, as much as the Foundation echos outwards towards us, we effect to the Foundation just as much. This soon established protocols for "Normalcy", while an incident causing the O5 Council to die except for O5-13. To help in their protection to the world, they create a machine that sets up and sorts the data of the Foundation. Whether it works, or the sentience that makes works on the machine, is what's hard to tell.

This time, when checking over possible end of the worlds, this time, a incident involving the degradation of reality, is all handled by a man who is split and fractured into three possibilities. All require the help of the Foundation in preventing the ultimate end of the world.

There are a few that do fit like during the Foundation's founding and creation, there was an accidental discovery of the Memetic Kill Agent, the discovery of "God's Blind Spot", Dead Men existing outside of reality, even having access to our wiki can be considered a anomaly, especially if there are malevolent gods.

- close

This is the best way I could describe some of the SCP-001 Proposals being a mixture of the origins of the Foundation, how parts of it are created, and how some of them are either world enders, or counter acting stuff to the anomalies. Some have even reached our reality, and it has reached back.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: Not necessarily unusual but In my headcanon a significant portion of the foundation personnel are anomalous. usually with one odd trait or so but enough to be beneficial; things like psionics, or memetic immunity etc etc.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
I see Yaldaboath as Tiamat/Gaia and The Broken God as Marduk/Chronos

This is an interesting take as in my favorite scip SCP-3895 which is implied to be yaldaboath is listed as a tiamat class, and the operation to counter it is called operation marduk. Nice connection.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: I tend to think that they don't kill off the D class every month. Eventually they are going to run out of bodies to leech off of the prison system.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: Ooh, I like this. Just the idea of the SCP Foundation having what could be the ultimate amnestic, something that could easily allow them to do major damage to GOIs or literally erase the memories of gods, but they don't know that and just have this powerful antimemetic weapon left in a concrete faraday cage for the rest of time.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: SCP- 055 is a amnestic enhanced to Very Fine in 914.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: This. This is amazing and a great unusual headcanon.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: There are trillions of anomalies in the SCP-verse at a minimum, of which the Foundation has about a million contained. Most of these are small annoying curses, phenomena, and hexes caused by interactions between anomalies. These are the main annoyance to the Foundation, making them have to figure out new workaround when anomalies make it so that no new SCP designations can end in 8 or that food turns conceptual in breakrooms. And that's just naturally-spawned anomalies, ignoring all the effects plaguing the Foundation that were created by bitter thaumaturges and demons and mages and more.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
Many SCPs are connected to mythology. I see Yaldaboath as Tiamat/Gaia and The Broken God as Marduk/Chronos, perspective changed the true stories.
SCP-3000 IS Anatashesha, but only it’s physical aspect. Also it is one of the heads of Yaldaboath.
The Scarlet King is an Eldritch Parasite (my next tale I’m hoping to propose :D )
SCP-343 is not at the foundation. But might be real.
SCP-2000 isn’t as effective as it’s made out to be, it can only restart humanity under a few K class scenarios.
SCP-2845 is a fragment of the broken god that is hostile to anything related to Yaldaboath. Including those who contain it.
SCP-610 is the primordial mud made from Yaldabolth’s corpse.
SCP-2718 is an info hazard that was only spread because bringing people back to life is forbidden, therefore when revived the subject experiences everything that happened to ones body prior to revival and then spreads the fear of death to others.
SCP-5000, 2718, and the Scarlet King are relevant to each other. (Also in my tale I want to write.)
Most SCP-001’s are true, but no one but the 05 council know which.
SCP-999 is the future savior of the universe.
SCP-173 is capable of killing most SCPs, because it is what inspired Enkidu from The Epic of Gilgamesh. Therefore it is a living paranormal weapon.
SCP-682 knows this and that’s why it was scared of it. Even though it can’t be killed by it.
The Foundation is aware of our existence.
SCP-049’s pestilence relates to the Scarlet King.
SCP-035 was also a weapon made by god-like entities.
Dr.Clef had a daughter with a nature goddess, and was given a final blessing against paranormal forces that negates their effects on him.
The factory is a tool of the Scarlet King.
The 05 council’s lifespan goes back to 1800s.
D-Class aren’t killed at the end of the month, but most left over are given to SCP-3000. The rest are given amenstics.
SCP-055… is still not round.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
Geddy Lee was, in fact, a prominent member of an Alagaddan cult prior to his containment by the Foundation. Certain theorists among the Foundation speculate his containment was achieved by vocal cord modification, hence why he sounds "normal" now.

Oh, hey! This inspired the final crosslink of SCP-5075, I never got to thank you for it.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
343 was the "forest spirit" who helped humanity defeat SCP-1000. The first humans he got to lead the revolution were Adam and Eve, who of course gave birth to 073 and 076.

SCP-096 was once a normal man living in the time of antiquity. He was incredibly vain and narcissistic. One day, he mocked an old woman for her appearance. Turns out, this woman was a witch, and she set a curse on him making sure that no one would be able to look at him. 096 laughed in her face and went back home. Once he went back to his village, the curse activated and he slaughtered all of them. He was fully conscious while it happened. He has been weeping ever since.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: I have to say: holy shit. I love every single bit of this.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
Foundation will still exist even 40.000 years from now with slight changes

No more hiding
SCP-001 is declassified and called "Empyrean" or "Warp"
SCP-001-A to -D are called "Chaos Gods"
Administrator is called "God-Emperor"
Overseers are called "Primarchs"
Foundation is now Inquisition
MTFs are called "Astartes Legions".

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: I like that one.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
The Department of Abnormalities is what the Foundation pins its failures and/or atrocities on. It's the Department that takes the blame for everything horrific that the Foundation has ever done. It does exist, but all information on it is just nonexistent.

In short, the Dept. of Abnormalities are the scapegoats.

My reasoning (Spoilers for 4182)
You've been warned

Author of 4182 mentions genocide denial, and the Dept. of Abnormalities being involved.

I interpret this to mean the Dept. of Abnormalities was involved in whatever atrocity created 4182. Or, at least, that's what they want you to think.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
Think about it: It's very likely that the 'O' in O5 stands for 'Overseer' but what about the 5?

I've heard that it indicates their security clearance level of 5, but I doubt the clearance level system was codified when the title was first created. Personally, I find the more amusing option being that their official title is five words beginning with O.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
Regarding what ihp said up there, some part of his interpretation does ring similar to mine; with the exception that there were only 5 overseers.

Think about it: It's very likely that the 'O' in O5 stands for 'Overseer' but what about the 5? That's a number that you just don't randomly pluck out like that and not make it have any other deeper meaning. Personally, I believe that there used to be 5 Overseers originally but gradually increased their numbers over time.

I also have the headcanon that O5-1 and The Administrator where once one and the same person. Given as a title but evolved over time.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: Tufto's Proposal is frickin godlike. I love how well written it is and how much of it is left vague and mysterious. All of the laws you have to figure out yourself and it provides some of the most interesting lore for the scarlet king I've ever seen.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
O5-1/The Wizard/Merlin

Currently held by a Rebecca Karim, a researcher in France.

ALLONS, ENFANTS DE LA PATRIIIIIIIIIIIE.
LE JOUR DE GLOIRE EEEEEEEST ARRIVEEEEEEEEEEEEE.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
TL;DR with two exceptions, the O5s are made up of just normal people throughout the world. More below the cut.

The Cut
Close the Cut

O5, Overseers, Overwatch, whatever you want to call them, are just normal people like you and me. In fact, they may be too normal— an algorithm selects them from a population of people who have next-to-no outstanding qualities for whatever their number is (more on that in a bit). These people don't try to rise above their means, show no ambition for upward or downward mobility, watch the Six o' Clock news each night, go to church on Sunday, mow their lawns, have two and a half kids, etc.

This is both a form of camouflage (after all, who's more likely to be an Overseer, the guy who travels everywhere with a heavily-armed motorcade or the woman who works behind the counter at a gas station?) and a way to ensure the Foundation and all the SCPs within it don't fall into the wrong hands. Would you rather have a president in charge of the Foundation? A king? A pope? A billionaire? If you want to protect humanity, you have to be ruled by it.

Each of the Numbers is occupied by a person fitting a particular archetype— 1 and 13 are unusual because they're picked from a pool of people in the know of the anomalous. The archetypes are as follows:

O5-1/The Wizard/Merlin: A member of the Foundation, promoted internally, but still keeps their normal jobs. Directors are never considered, due to the fact that they tend to abuse power. Currently held by a Rebecca Karim, a researcher in France.

O5-2/The Skeptic/Houdini: Someone who is critical of the existence of the supernatural. There to make sure everyone shaves with Occam's razor regularly, as well as be a general voice of reason. Usually an older individual, currently held by Richard Parson, a freelance writer for several scientific magazines

O5-3/The Pariah/Underground Man: Someone who is utterly despised or excluded by the group they are part of. There to give counsel on how the Foundation's actions can affect the downtrodden and less-fortunate of the world, and be a voice of criticism against the elite. Currently held by a beggar in India.

O5-4/The Rebel/Lucifer: The youngest of the Overseers, this number can be held only by individuals between the ages of 25 and 35; once they age out, they are permitted to hand-pick the next individual in this slot. They're a voice of dissent and passion among the Overseers, as their youth still burns bright enough to get through the cynicism many of them have settled into. Currently held by Alicia Sykes, a barista for a Starbucks in Michigan.

O5-5/The Scientist/Newton: As their name would suggest, an academic type, typically a professor or other knowledgeable individual. Typically not an expert in any one field, but knows from a broad array of subjects, and can easily learn more thanks to the Foundation's paratech. Currently held by Min-Seo Song, a professor at Korea University.

O5-6/The Doctor/Aesclepius: A medical professional, advises on the health effects the Foundation's actions could have on the rest of the world. Typically of higher value than the other O5s, the Foundation will prioritize protecting O5-6 above any others if need be. Currently held by Dr. Kayin Afolayan, who works in a medical clinic in West Africa.

O5-7/The Zealot/Peter: Typically the oldest and most conservative of the O5s, chosen for strong religious convictions, meant to consider the theological consequences of the Foundation's actions. Typically not held by someone of a non-Abrhamic faith. Currently held by Father Jack O'Rourke, an Australian preacher.

O5-8/The Soldier/Ares: Other than 1 and 13, 8 is the only number occupied by someone who's not a general civilian; as the name suggests, they're a member of a military force. The Foundation ensures that they avoid active combat zones as much as possible, but recognize that experience is necessary for a good soldier. Currently held by Archikelefstis(Chief Petty Officer) Niko Ioannidis of the Greek Navy.

O5-9/The Farmer/Demeter: Not necessarily a literal farmer, but someone who works with the supply and demand chain of food— fishermen, food scientists, and even chefs have held this position. Directs the Foundation on ethical concerns regarding the food supply of the planet. Currently held by Captain Ian Freize, a Fisherman.

O5-10/The Historian/Epimetheus: Those who do not remember history are doomed to repeat it. O5-10 is here to make sure that doesn't happen, having access to the most complete record of the Foundation's actions and consequences at their disposal. Not necessarily a literal historian, as it is currently held by Jasmine McGrath, a computer programmer.

O5-11/The Hedonist/de Sade: The closest the Council will have to an 'evil' member, if the Ethics Committee is doing their job right. Cares only about themselves and their life, everything else is inconsequential, but will ultimately vote to preserve everything that lets them continue having the good time that is their life. Typically held by a criminal, in this case, Antonio DiSilva of the Italian Mob.

O5-12/The Wild Card/Loki: What makes this position unique is that nobody except the Ethics Committee knows who's assigned to this position. Some of the O5s think that it's an AI that just spits out a random vote, while others think it's a position that rotates between viable candidates annually, if not on a per-meeting basis.

O5-13/The Spy/Judas: None of the O5s choose to be O5s, but 13 least of all. They are kidnapped and forcibly recruited from other groups of interest, usually brainwashed in the process. O5-13 typically only acts as a tiebreaker vote, but rarely, they do speak up. Currently held by Porrox, an agent of a Proto-Sarkic revivalist sect in eastern Europe.

(Furthermore, in this interpretation, SCP-006 doesn't exist, because fuck free elixirs of immortality.)

Apologies for the rambling, this came to me late at night and I had to write it.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
Oh boy this is a fun can of worms to open.

So here's my basic head cannon 4 Originals, Abstracts, Gods, Minor Gods, Humans, and then Originals in a new universe of the end of this one.

Lets get started

+ show block
– hide block

Abstracts exist above these gods like the Scarlet King and the ones who names I can't spell. But even these gods are way beyond humans. So why do they take an interest in humans?

I think 682 is a minor God, during the war where the Chaos King(I mean Scarlet King) attacked the tree of knowledge they failed. 682 was supposed to destroy its roots. So the Scarlet King punished him by placing him in our reality as a shell of his former glory. He might be a child of the Scarlet King or just a minor God that sided with him as others did.

I think the gods are taking a strange interest in humans. Giving them power and making them monsters. But somehow humans can make themselves monsters as well?

So I see it like this the abstracts and gods were made by 4 Originals(one was naughty so they go by 3). They created the abstracts which created the gods. Gods created realms and minor gods. But nothing created Earth and humans. This is why we're special to them, first these 3 Originals noticed us, then the abstracts, then the gods. Humans have a potential. Some want to nourish it, some like Scarlet King and the fleshy one want to abuse and enslave it.

I think there are also entities that are minor gods that help create anomalies as well here on Earth. They do it as a way of giving us the means to protect ourselves when the next war starts. The Foundation imprisions all of them, but should be encouraging and developing certain individuals abilities instead.

I see SCPs lining up with minor gods. The flesh that hates, the fleshy one that might have been born of Instinct. 173 the Broken God, probably corrupted, but it's in line with his thing, the Broken god probably was born from Knowledge. 682 is a servant and possible child of The Scarlet King. 096 is probably either a creation or servant of the fleshy bitch that broke the Broken God. The Scarlet King was probably born from either Death, Chaos, or Hate. But he's pretty complex so I could totally see him being born minor god like 682 or 096 and then consuming powers of others until reaching his state.

Then there's a 4th original that makes bad things, abstracts like Greed, Envy, and such and maybe had a hand in the Scarlet Kings rise to power.

Now that brings us back to Earth and humans. Why the interest?

Well in my head cannon no one created Earth it just happened. Even the Originals can't see its future. And decide to take interest in a short tale I wrote that's quickly getting down voted but you can look at my sandbox if you want, I'm not going to promote the tale here. But they take interest because we can do what they can, create but they didn't make us and can tell none of their creations did either. So they decide to help and see where we end up. The Originals paying attention to mankind, draws the attention of the Abstracts, which after their war, draws the attention of the gods.

I think the losing side sees potential in humans, maybe Scarlet King sees his failure in the fact that other gods had their own needs and powers and desires. But with humans he can take them and twist them to his will and even pull them away from time and space as willing slaves to be turned into powerful weapons and soldiers. The same with Fleshy girl. I think the Broken God has better intentions but is doing the same in his own way, also since he's broken it would seem his power is kinda easy to get if you know how. Of course humans seem able to tap into this power as well, changing themselves into monsters, maybe a gift from the 4th Original?

I created a character called the Blacksmith, people with one powerful drive in their soul find their way to him. He can shape their soul into anything, as souls are very powerful. But if you give him your mind body and soul he can make basically a minor god killing level weapon, shield, or item. He does this so mankind can stand on its own in this eventual multidimensional war 2.

Any immortal SCP on earth is either a minor God or created by a minor God whether they know it or not, but not necessarily immortal in a higher realm. 096 is minor God level character probably created by flesh girl. Able is probably a creation of a death god or war god, he may even be a creation of an abstract like Hate, or Death. Though I think abstracts would be more hands off unless something really caught their eye. If Able was the first to be murdered maybe Hate would take notice in him and give him power.

Only other thing that I see specifically is 682 is Saint George's Dragon, but he is killable, or banishable at least, by an anomaly or person with anomalous powers. Saint George was one. He beat 682 and that's why it took 682 so long to come back, I think the earliest story on site is 1890s in France?

Either way he was able to defeat or, at least in an earthly since, kill the Unkillable Lizard, but like I said he's from a higher realm this form is just a shell. So I imagine for him it was like FREEDOM and then the Scarlett King tortured his ass and sent him back. Or maybe Saint George was simply able to contain him or hurt him bad enough to cause him to take that long to recover.

Basically these gods are turning humans into new things and having them join there side for the upcoming war, whether they twist them into something new(the flesh that hates), gift them powers(any human SCP), or simply take their unusually powerful souls for themselves(cult sacrifices), they want humans.

I also see all anomalous creatures as relating to one of these gods or maybe abstracts. Though I think abstracts would be much less directly involved. So fairies were connected to something, as they hated the factory(which was something probably corrupted from the Broken God) fairies were probably made by his enemy.

Basically every time I read or watch a new SCP(on YouTube) I cant help but wonder how it fits into the wider universe. Who made it? Why? Where did they get the power from? Is it for humans or for these gods' war that's destined to repeat.

This all started when I read the Library and tree of knowledge where the same and there was a war to destroy it, led by the Scarlet King. If they were the same than something bigger than these gods made it, like Abstracts, which came from something bigger than them, the 4 Originals. And instead of going higher I thought why not just make it a loop. Humans could end up as the creators of the next universe or the destroyers of this one.

So that's my weird take on the background of the SCP universe.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: Might have already posted here, but maybe 049-J and dado are the same person.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: Hm. If we're actually considering this, I believe their place would be at the Department of the Preservation of Reality (aka the SCPFoundation one). They would perhaps be a backup force to support the other organizations, an escort team as you said.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: To my knowledge there's not really an analog to a Stand magic system in the Foundationverse, so maybe they'd function more as an escort/resource provision organization? That seems to be their primary function in the anime anyways.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
A reference to JOJO? *me likes*

Robert Speedwagon's crew would be the perfect addition to the Leadership… :D

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: Perhaps the biggest flaw in this theory is the absence of the Speedwagon Foundation.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
My headcanon is… indeed unusual. I have combined all the fictional anomaly - containment agencies and I have come up with the following :

  • There is one large organization that litteraly rules the world. There's no name mentioned anywhere, so I just call it "The Leadership"
  • It's divided in Departments. The Department in which the Foundation belongs is the Department of the Preservation of Reality (DePR)
  • The DePR is made up of MONARCH (from the Godzilla films), MIB (from the films with the same name) and the Foundation. The first one takes care of non-anomalous but not explainable by science MUTOs, the second hides the extraterrestrial visitors of the Earth, and the Foundation… well, contains anomalies (!)
  • The other Departments are the Department of Misinformation (which is made up of the Consortium from Inferno and the Assassins Clan from Assassin's Creed), the Department of External Affairs (made up of the High Table from the John Wick films, the Council of External Relations, a IRL "secret" organization, and the Freemasons, because why not), and the Department of Control (the one that rules the Leadership)

TL;DR: The Foundation is only part of something much, much bigger. How much, we'll never learn.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
Apologies for the necropost:

I tend to think the possible long-term effects of SCPs would be one reason why the Foundation wouldn't actually terminate D-classes. If they are released with amenstics, it would be relatively simple for the Foundation, via governments, to keep tabs on any unusual developments amongst the group, and pull in any D-class for analysis. It would also make experimental design easier.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
As archivists begin to work for the Foundation and read over SCP files, they become more and more hopeless as the realizations of imminent global extinction and the unethical actions of the Foundation set in. To cope with this, they write their own fictional documentation that mocks these fears. They create a world where death is inconsequential and ethics is a suggestion. They admit the faults of the Foundation openly, and laugh.

They create Joke articles.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
After looking at this thread three times, I'm convinced that this is a perfect source of inspiration for made fanfictions, some of the theories are pretty inspiring.

Anyway, it's not unusual, but in my headcanon, dado looks like that and he's a really intelligent person who pretends to be stupid and so, the Foundation and the other GoIs think that he's not a threat. And it works.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: The Foundation acts like the US Government, with three branches. The Administrator conducts outreach with governments, and can push executive orders like the president. O5 Council is like Congress, but with military control as well, and the Ethics Committee is like the Supreme Court.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: Ah, I see. So the way it's named as crowning is more of what they want, ideal goal, than what's actually going on. In other words, it's indeed named in respect of the power of the object.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
I just don't think sometging like "We kept it in cage for 15 seconds, then realized the cage is useless." Is "an achievement to be celebrated".

I just think the Foundation has higher standard than that.

MacLeod has already stated that "successfully contained Keter anomalies are the ones that are referred to as "crowning achievements of containment.""

You seem to be convinced that he stated that all Keter procedures are crowning achievements, regardless of containment reliability.

That said, not all Keter-class anomalies have "highly ineffective or unreliable procedures". Keter refers to the difficulty and complexity of containment moreso than how much the Foundation is struggling to contain those things (which Apollyon is probably a better example of, since if I recall correctly, it's the ultimate in containment futility).

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
I just don't think sometging like "We kept it in cage for 15 seconds, then realized the cage is useless." Is "an achievement to be celebrated".

Unless your point is that the name is originated from mislead personnel who celebrate too early, then I"m aware of that possibility, I just usually don't see the Foundation's personnel with that level of idiocy.

But this is about your headcanon anyway, so this conversation goes too long already. If you think the Foundation's personnel are like gamers who think "Killed by Level 1 Boss" is an achievement worthy of celebrate (and I perfectly understand that, if the game is really hard then it's indeed a step of improvement), then who I'm to argue? I just think the Foundation has higher standard than that.

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded: You are severely missing my point.
